title: Law Enforcement Officer and Firefighter Recreation Pass Act
short_title: Law Enforcement Officer and Firefighter Recreation Pass Act
plain_summary: This bill creates free passes for law enforcement officers and firefighters to visit national parks and other federal recreation areas. These workers would get the same type of free annual passes that military members already receive. To get a free pass, law enforcement officers and firefighters must show proof that they work in these jobs. The passes would allow them to visit national parks and federal recreation lands without paying the usual entrance fees.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Law Enforcement Officer and Firefighter Recreation Pass Act.

sections.title: Section 2  National Recreational Passes for Law Enforcement Officers and Firefighters
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Federal Lands Recreation Enhancement Act. It changes the rules about who can get free annual passes to national parks and federal recreation areas. The new rules say that the government must give free annual passes to members of the military and their family members. The government must also give free passes to law enforcement officers and firefighters who can prove they are eligible. The Secretary of the Interior will decide what proof people need to show. The law explains that a firefighter means any government employee at the federal, state, local, or tribal level whose job involves putting out fires, including wildfires. A law enforcement officer means any government officer, agent, or employee at the federal, state, local, or tribal level who is legally allowed to prevent crime, investigate crimes, or supervise people who have been convicted of crimes.
